mechanics.text:
RUNNING THE GAME
From
dramatic   perspective, introducing these important NPCs should vary from the norm t0 let the players know that they have met someone who stands out from the rest of the back- ground. Maybe the way these individuals talk; 0r the way they carry themselves_ Maybe they are bold and walk right UD to the player characters when everyone else snies away: they have been stalking tne players for some time and only now allowed themselves t0 be noticed However accomplished the player characters should probably know when they have met somconc important, lest the entire effort creating the character be wast: ed as they dismiss this fully developed NPC out of hand
The character' $ Secondary Skill should be for secondary weapon (if combatant) hobby Interest Skill (for Mon combatants). The Secondary Skill mignt be something unusual such as Martial Arts Stealth or Technician depending on where the NPC encountered and under what circumstances All characters have Language and Perception Skills these are not listed unless the character has an unusually high skill bonus_ Ada them becomes necessary:
Bystander This character one of the faceless trillions who fill every inhabited planet; Though rarely threat from combat point of view. bystanders might be held as hostages needing rescue, serve as particularly poor henchmen for bottom-rung criminals
MINOR NPCS The vast majority of NPCs encountered by the player char- aclers minor NPCs. from the Buv who sold you that Sternsnacht pistol to tne waiter at the loca bistro, from tne Eruff sergeant to the hapless Street punk who points the way t0 the yakuza hide-out. In most cases minoi NPCs need only personality and briel pnysica description: they possess usetul skills tnat are likely t0 come into play. the gamemaster should note tnese as well: Make sure the basic motivations each minor NPC are figured out before the player characters meet them; that way making consistent decisions for them will be easier; For exam: you know ahead ot time that particular merchant iS money grubbing penny pincher but iS also collector of Star League relics, YOU will know that he iS unlikely t0 be flexible haegling unless tne player characters bnng piece of lostech t0 the bargaining table Rarely does significant threat lie in single individual with henchmen . Wnile many NPCs will require no real game stats, the enemy soldiers security guards. hired thugs and irate bar patrons whom tne player characters run afoul of will need some numbers to back up their combat threat; always helps t0 have game stats for these kinds 0f characters ready before the game so the play doesn" grind t0 halt while you page through the rules To save ou the trouble coming up with all those num: bers yourself; few pre-Eenerated generic NPCs that can fit in variety of situations are presented below. Each one has few built-in options to be customized by the gamemaster depending exactly whom they represent: Fecl tree add special skills and equipment as needed for the adventure at hand (not all minor NPCs will be armed but each type is supplied with equip ment appropriate to his station in case he used as com batanti; Each minor NPC is listed with Primary Skill. Tnis will be different skill depending on what kind of character In the case combatant characters Ine Primary Skill will weapon skill matching whatever the character listed as hav- ing (the top weapon the Iist there are severall: character armed with pistol would use Pistols as his Primary Skill; Non- combatant characters would instead use the Primary Skill for something related to their Jobs: ior examdie_ salesperson might have Negotiation as his Primary Skill,
